page: Microsoft Azure M16ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MS server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. A key hardware characteristic is its substantial memory capacity of 438.0 GB, yielding 27.34 GB of RAM per core. The server includes 524 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not feature any dedicated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a clear trade-off between raw processing speed and memory capacity. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the weak and poor categories respectively. Single-threaded compression tasks also register in the bottom decile. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth is stable, with cached read speeds reaching 610.54 GB/sec and uncached read speeds at 73.69 GB/sec, both placing in the average tier. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations at 10.46 million operations per second, also perform at an average level.

From a cost efficiency perspective, the M16ms offers high memory density relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for workloads that require massive RAM footprints without demanding top-tier CPU compute power. The hardware configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and enterprise applications requiring significant memory allocations.
description: Microsoft Azure M16ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS family, designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features an Intel Xeon 8280M x86_64 processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 2.7 GHz clock speed. The hardware is characterized by a high memory capacity of 438.0 GB, alongside 524 GB of local storage, with no integrated GPUs. Benchmark data reveals a trade-off: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression are weak, while multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at average levels. Qualitatively, the server provides high memory density relative to compute resources, making it cost-efficient for memory-bound tasks rather than compute-heavy processing. It is suited for large databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M16ms is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 438 GB of RAM, and 524 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M16ms is a memory-optimized server with 16 Intel vCPUs, 438 GB RAM, and 524 GB local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density and local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

438.0 GB of RAM providing high memory-to-vCPU density
524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Suited for large databases, in-memory caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications
